brief.headline_en: UPC Tightens Evidence Rules as PMAC Mediation System Launches
brief.summary_zh: 2026年Q2，UPC案件量持续攀升，杜塞尔多夫增设合议庭以应对积压。法院通过系列判例确立严格证据披露规则及审查档案对权利要求的限制作用，同时PMAC调解系统正式上线。中国跃居EPO申请来源国前三，需警惕UPC“全有或全无”风险，利用新机制优化维权策略。
key_points: 程序与证据趋严：UPC明确初步异议仅限管辖权等程序事项，严格执行证据前置披露，迟交证据易被排除；审查档案陈述将限制权利要求解释范围。
ADR机制落地：PMAC程序规则获批且案件管理系统上线，为SEP/FRAND及单一专利纠纷提供官方调解仲裁渠道，有助于降低跨境维权成本。
机构扩容与数据透明：杜塞尔多夫地院设立第二合议庭缓解案件压力；UPC恢复月度统计发布，显示德法荷分院仍为诉讼集中地，法律确定性逐步增强。
actions: 复盘专利撰写质量：确保EPO审查阶段陈述与诉讼策略一致，避免在UPC诉讼中因审查档案禁反言导致保护范围被狭义解读。
建立证据合规流程：针对UPC严格的证据披露时限，提前整理技术来源证明与实物证据链，避免因程序瑕疵导致关键证据失权。
评估PMAC调解机会：对于涉及SEP或复杂侵权纠纷，优先评估通过PMAC进行调解或仲裁的可能性，以规避UPC一审的高昂成本与不确定性。
watchlist: UPC上诉法院关于管辖权范围提请欧盟法院裁决的最终结果
2027年EPO指南修订中关于修改依据强制义务的具体落地细则
中国企业在UPC作为原告或被告的典型判例及其对等同原则的适用倾向
brief.summary_en: In Q2 2026, the Unified Patent Court (UPC) intensified procedural rigor while expanding alternative dispute resolution capabilities. The Düsseldorf Local Division established a second panel to manage rising caseloads, and monthly statistics resumed, confirming Germany, France, and the Netherlands as primary litigation hubs. Jurisprudence now strictly limits preliminary objections to jurisdictional matters, enforces early evidence disclosure, and applies file wrapper estoppel to claim interpretation. Concurrently, the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) launched its case management system, offering formal mediation for SEP/FRAND and unitary patent disputes. With China entering the top three EPO filing sources, stakeholders must navigate the UPC’s all-or-nothing enforcement risks by aligning prosecution histories with litigation strategies and leveraging new ADR mechanisms to mi...
key_points_en: Procedural strictness increases: Preliminary objections are restricted to jurisdiction; late evidence faces exclusion, and prosecution history estoppel narrows claim scope.
ADR infrastructure operational: PMAC rules are adopted and its case management system is live, providing official channels for mediating complex cross-border and SEP disputes.
Capacity and transparency grow: Düsseldorf adds a second judicial panel to reduce backlogs, while resumed statistical reporting highlights sustained activity in key European divisi...
actions_en: Audit patent prosecution histories to ensure EPO responses align with litigation positions, preventing narrow claim interpretation via file wrapper estoppel.
Implement strict evidence compliance protocols to meet UPC disclosure deadlines, securing technical proof chains early to avoid procedural preclusion.
Evaluate PMAC mediation for SEP or complex infringement matters to potentially bypass high-cost UPC litigation and achieve faster, confidential resolutions.
watchlist_en: Final outcome of the UPC Court of Appeal referral to the CJEU regarding jurisdictional scope.
Specific implementation details in the 2027 EPO Guidelines concerning mandatory indication of amendment bases.
Emerging precedents involving Chinese entities in UPC courts, particularly regarding the application of the doctrine of equivalents.

items.title: SACEPO WP/G discusses outcomes of user consultation on the Guidelines 2026
items.title_zh: 欧专局讨论2026指南用户咨询结果及2027修订方向
items.title_en: EPO SACEPO Reviews 2026 Guidelines Consultation and Outlines 2027 Updates
items.summary: The SACEPO WP/G members met to discuss the responses to the public user consultation on the EPC, PCT-EPO, and the UP Guidelines
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧专局SACEPO工作组于5月12日会议讨论2026版审查指南（含EPC、PCT-EPO及统一专利指南）的用户咨询反馈。咨询共获307条意见，主要关注EPC指南。会议还展望2027版更新，包括支持无纸化流程的法律变更、可能强制要求指明修改依据，以及引入统一专利法院上诉法院判例。此外，讨论了G 1/24决定实施、抗体创造性、分案申请及异议程序等议题。
items.ai_summary_en: The SACEPO Working Party on Guidelines discussed feedback from the public consultation on the 2026 EPC, PCT-EPO, and Unitary Patent Guidelines. The consultation received 307 comments, primarily from European patent representatives focusing on the EPC Guidelines. The EPO outlined planned updates for the 2027 edition, supporting the transition to a fully paperless granting process by April 2027. Proposed changes include potential obligations to indicate the basis for amendments during European phase entry and opposition proceedings. Additionally, the EPO intends to incorporate references to Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal decisions into the EPC Guidelines. Discussions also covered the implementation of Enlarged Board of Appeal decision G 1/24 and ongoing considerations for G 1/25.
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧专局正通过指南修订强化程序简化与质量管控，特别是适应无纸化转型及统一专利法院判例体系。从业者需密切关注2027版指南关于修改依据指明的潜在义务，以及UPC判例对EPC审查实践的具体影响，同时留意抗体可专利性及分案异议程序的后续澄清。
items.ai_insight_en: Practitioners should prepare for procedural shifts in the 2027 Guidelines, particularly regarding mandatory amendment bases and UPC case law integration. The inclusion of UPC decisions signals closer alignment between EPO practice and unitary patent jurisprudence. Monitoring the final text on amendment requirements is essential for drafting strategies in European phase entries and oppositions.

The 2027 Guidelines will support full paperless processing and may mandate indicating amendment bases in specific proceedings.
EPO plans to reference UPC Court of Appeal decisions in the EPC Guidelines and clarify G 1/24 implementation.

Practice note: Anticipate stricter formal requirements for indicating amendment bases during European phase entry and opposition proceedings under the 2027 revisions.
What to monitor: Final wording of the 2027 Guidelines regarding G 1/25 implementation and the specific scope of UPC case law references.

items.title: Unified Patent Court published the 2025 annual report
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院发布2025年度报告
items.title_en: Unified Patent Court Publishes 2025 Annual Report Highlighting Case Management System Launch
items.summary: The UPC published its second annual report
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）发布第二份年度报告，回顾其成立三年来的活动、案件量及关键成就。报告强调新案件管理系统（CMS）的成功上线是重要里程碑，体现了与欧洲专利局及用户组织的紧密合作。法院高层指出，UPC已巩固其作为领先知识产权诉讼论坛的地位，致力于在技术快速进步背景下提供高质量、及时的判决，并持续加强判例法发展及利益相关方沟通。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) has released its second annual report, reviewing activities, caseload, and key achievements from 2025. The report highlights the successful launch of a new Case Management System as a major milestone, demonstrating effective cooperation between the UPC, the European Patent Office, and user organizations. Court leadership emphasized the institution's growing position as a leading IP litigation forum, focusing on developing case law, ensuring transparency, and delivering high-quality decisions within competitive timeframes despite an increasing workload.
items.ai_insight_zh: UPC运作日趋成熟，诉讼效率与透明度提升将增加其在欧专利纠纷中的吸引力。中国企业应密切关注其判例法发展及新系统对程序的影响，优化在欧洲的专利布局与维权策略，利用其高效机制应对潜在侵权风险或发起进攻性诉讼。
items.ai_insight_en: The publication of the second annual report signals the UPC's transition into a mature judicial institution with established procedural frameworks. For rights holders and legal practitioners, the emphasis on the new Case Management System and consistent delivery of judgments suggests increasing procedural predictability. This development reinforces the UPC's viability as a central venue for European patent disputes, warranting close attention to its evolving jurisprudence and administrative capabilities.

items.title: Demand for European patents in 2025 exceeded 200 000 for first time
items.title_zh: 2025年欧洲专利申请量首超20万件
items.title_en: EPO Patent Filings Exceed 200,000 in 2025 as China Enters Top Three
items.summary: EPO Technology Dashboard shows strong growth in demand for patents for digital and energy technologies. The US and Germany remain top countries of origin, while China enters top three for first time.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局数据显示，2025年专利申请量达201,974件，同比增长1.4%，首次突破20万件大关。数字与能源技术领域增长强劲，计算机技术居首，AI与量子技术增速显著。美国与德国为最大申请来源国，中国首次进入前三。统一专利 uptake 较高，尤其在欧洲专利权人及中小企业中表现突出。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office recorded a historic 201,974 patent applications in 2025, marking a 1.4% annual increase and the first time filings surpassed 200,000. The US and Germany remained the leading countries of origin, while China entered the top three for the first time. Growth was driven by digital and energy technologies, with computer technology leading overall. Significant uptake of the Unitary Patent was observed, particularly among European patentees and SMEs. The EPO highlighted strong performance in AI, quantum technologies, and battery innovations, noting that these trends help identify priority areas for strengthening European tech sovereignty and competitiveness within an integrated innovation market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 专利申请总量创新高且中国进入前三，反映全球竞争格局变化。统一专利在中小企业中渗透率提升，显示其降低壁垒成效。AI、量子及6G相关技术成为增长引擎，企业应关注这些战略领域的布局动态及欧洲技术主权政策导向。
items.ai_insight_en: The milestone volume underscores Europe’s appeal as a global technology market, with the Unitary Patent accelerating market integration. Strategic sectors like AI and semiconductors show distinct regional strengths, guiding portfolio allocation. Rights holders should note the shifting competitive landscape as Asian applicants increase their presence in core European technology fields.

China joined the US and Germany in the top three countries of origin for European patent applications.
Computer technology led filings, with strong growth in AI, quantum technologies, and digital communication sectors.

Practice note: Computer technology remains the dominant field, but rapid growth in quantum and AI sub-fields suggests increasing competition for specialized technical claims.
What to monitor: Track filing trends from applicants from China in digital communication and energy sectors as they solidify their position among top origins.

items.title: Select Committee commends strong Unitary Patent uptake and SME engagement
items.title_zh: 专委肯定统一专利强劲增长及中小企业参与
items.title_en: EPO Select Committee Reports Strong Unitary Patent Uptake and SME Engagement
items.summary: In their meeting on 19 March 2026, the Select Committee of the Administrative Council of the EPO reviewed a variety of topics and welcomed the steadily increasing uptake of the Unitary Patent by patentees around the world, with Eu
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年3月19日，EPO行政理事会专委会议肯定统一专利 uptake 稳步增长，2025年达28.7%，欧洲用户为主力，其中超40%为中小企业。UPC案件量增加，英语为主要程序语言，财务状况良好。UPC上诉法院确认EPO登记实践，并首次向欧盟法院提请澄清管辖权问题。EPO协助欧盟委员会更新统一专利运行报告，证实系统实施顺利。
items.ai_summary_en: The EPO Administrative Council’s Select Committee reviewed the Unitary Patent system on 19 March 2026, noting steadily increasing uptake driven primarily by European users. By 2025, the uptake rate reached 28.7%, with over 86,000 requests filed since launch. Small and medium-sized enterprises account for more than 40% of European proprietors. The Unified Patent Court reported a rising caseload, with English as the dominant procedural language and limited need for Member State financing. The Committee also adopted modernized governance measures and acknowledged the EPO’s contribution to the European Commission’s upcoming report, confirming the system’s smooth legal and operational implementation.
items.ai_insight_en: The data confirms the Unitary Patent is maturing as a cost-effective option for diverse rights holders, particularly SMEs. The dominance of English in UPC proceedings may influence litigation preparation for non-English speakers. Continued monitoring of the European Commission’s final report is advisable for assessing long-term regulatory stability.

core_points_en: Unitary Patent uptake reached 28.7% in 2025, with over 86,000 total requests filed since the system launched.
European users drive demand, accounting for approximately 60% of requests, with SMEs representing over 40% of European proprietors.

What to monitor: Track the European Commission’s forthcoming report on Unitary Patent regulations for any identified operational challenges or proposed legislative adjustments.

items.title: European Patent Office meets with German Association for Intellectual Property Law
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局与德国知识产权法协会举行双边会谈
items.title_en: EPO and GRUR Discuss Quality Plan, Unitary Patent System, and Strategic Foresight
items.summary: EPO-GRUR bilateral meeting on 2 March 2026: Recent developments, quality, unitary patent system and the Observatory were among topics discussed.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年3月2日，欧洲专利局（EPO）在慕尼黑与德国知识产权法协会（GRUR）举行双边会谈。双方讨论了EPO《2026年质量行动计划》、统一专利（UP）及统一专利法院（UPC）的最新进展，以及EPO专利与技术观察局的战略前瞻工作。GRUR肯定了EPO的服务质量及UP系统的成功，双方同意继续加强合作以提升单一市场技术主权。
items.ai_summary_en: On 2 March 2026, the European Patent Office hosted a bilateral meeting with the German Association for Intellectual Property Law (GRUR) in Munich. Led by EPO Vice-President Christoph Ernst and GRUR President Dr Anke Nordemann-Schiffel, discussions focused on the EPO’s Quality Action Plan 2026, emphasizing examiner training and AI-driven digital transformation. The parties reviewed the operational success of the Unitary Patent system and the Unified Patent Court, aligning with broader EU goals for technological sovereignty. GRUR representatives commended the EPO’s service quality and reliability. The dialogue also covered strategic foresight initiatives, reinforcing the commitment to structured user engagement and continued cooperation on key developments within the European patent landscape.
items.ai_insight_en: This dialogue underscores the EPO’s prioritization of quality consistency and digital integration amid the maturing Unitary Patent system. For practitioners, the explicit endorsement of AI tools suggests evolving examination workflows that may impact response strategies. The strong alignment between the EPO and major user associations like GRUR signals stable procedural expectations, while the focus on strategic foresight indicates long-term policy adjustments based on emerging technology trends.

items.title: EPO President delivers keynote at INTA Annual Meeting in London
items.title_zh: EPO主席在伦敦INTA年会发表主旨演讲
items.title_en: EPO President Advocates Holistic IP Strategy at INTA Annual Meeting in London
items.summary: EPO President Campinos delivered a keynote at the 2026 INTA Annual Meeting in London on 5 May, calling for a more holistic approach to intellectual property in which patents and trademarks work together.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）主席坎皮诺斯在2026年伦敦INTA年会上发表主旨演讲，倡导专利与商标协同的整体知识产权策略。他强调高质量专利对投资确定性的重要性，指出欧洲专利申请约五个月即可获得检索报告。此外，他重点推介统一专利制度，称其为覆盖18个欧盟成员国的简便途径，中小企业申请占比约40%，并提及EPO与INTA在人工智能及标准必要专利等领域的合作前景。
items.ai_summary_en: EPO President António Campinos delivered a keynote at the 2026 INTA Annual Meeting in London, urging a holistic approach where patents and trademarks function synergistically within business strategy. He highlighted that startups combining both IP rights are significantly more likely to secure funding and achieve successful exits. Campinos emphasized the role of high-quality patents in providing legal certainty, noting that European applicants receive search reports and preliminary opinions within approximately five months. He also promoted the Unitary Patent as an accessible route for protection across 18 EU Member States, with SMEs accounting for around 40% of requests for unitary effect, underscoring its value for market entry and investment.
items.ai_insight_en: The EPO’s emphasis on integrating patent and trademark strategies reflects a broader shift toward viewing IP as a unified business asset rather than siloed legal rights. For in-house counsel, this reinforces the need for cross-functional IP management to maximize valuation and investor appeal. The high SME adoption of the Unitary Patent suggests it is becoming a standard tool for cost-effective European coverage. Monitoring the evolving dialogue between the EPO and INTA on AI and standard-essential patents may reveal future convergence in examination and enforcement practices.

items.title: EPO hosts annual bilateral meeting with AIPLA
items.title_zh: EPO与AIPLA举行年度双边会议
items.title_en: EPO and AIPLA Discuss Unitary Patent Uptake, Quality Plan, and Pending Referral G1/25
items.summary: The EPO and AIPLA strengthen collaboration through exchanges on quality, digital transformation, the Unitary Patent, the UPC and developments in EPO practice
items.ai_summary_zh: 3月26日，欧洲专利局（EPO）在海牙总部接待美国知识产权法协会（AIPLA）代表团。双方就专利质量、数字化转型、统一专利（UP）及统一专利法院（UPC）进展展开对话。EPO强调通过“2026质量行动计划”提升审查一致性与法律确定性。数据显示，2025年美国申请人提交的UP请求增长28%，占非EPC申请人的16.6%。会议还讨论了待决转介G1/25及抗体发明审查实践。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) and the American Intellectual Property Law Association (AIPLA) held their annual bilateral meeting in The Hague on 26 March 2026. Discussions centered on the EPO’s Quality Action Plan 2026, digital transformation initiatives, and legal developments, including the pending referral G1/25 regarding description adaptation. The parties highlighted the growing strategic relevance of the Unitary Patent (UP) and Unified Patent Court (UPC). Data presented indicated that US applicants remain the largest group of non-EPC filers for UPs, accounting for 16.6% of requests following a 28% increase in uptake during 2025. The dialogue also covered recent changes in examination practice for antibody-related inventions and the continued strong use of the EPO validation system by US rights holders.
items.ai_insight_en: The significant rise in Unitary Patent filings by US applicants signals increasing confidence in the unitary system among transatlantic rights holders. For practitioners, the EPO’s emphasis on the Quality Action Plan 2026 and specific updates on antibody examination suggest a continued focus on legal certainty and technical precision. Monitoring the outcome of referral G1/25 is essential, as it may impact drafting standards for description adaptation across European portfolios.

items.title: EPO meets with the German Association of Industry IP Experts
items.title_zh: EPO与德国工业知识产权专家协会举行双边会晤
items.title_en: EPO and VPP Discuss Unitary Patent Adoption, AI Integration, and German IP Strategy
items.summary: EPO-VPP bilateral meeting on 25 March 2026: Recent developments, increasing IP awareness, AI, quality were among topics discussed.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年3月25日，欧洲专利局（EPO）在慕尼黑会见德国工业知识产权专家协会（VPP）代表团。双方就欧洲专利体系发展、统一专利制度进展、人工智能在专利审查中的应用及质量提升等议题展开讨论。VPP支持德国国家知识产权战略概念，并肯定EPO在促进技术转移和支持中小企业方面的努力，同时强调需兼顾德国产业特定利益。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office hosted the German Association of Industry IP Experts (VPP) in Munich to align on patent system developments. Discussions covered the Unitary Patent’s growing uptake among SMEs, with 2025 data showing significant participation from smaller entities. The parties examined the human-centric integration of AI into operations and supported using EPO data tools for Germany’s national IP strategy. While acknowledging the Unitary Patent’s value, VPP emphasized balancing these benefits with specific German industrial interests. The meeting reinforced collaboration on fostering technology transfer, empowering startups, and maintaining quality standards within a sustainable innovation ecosystem.
items.ai_insight_en: This dialogue highlights the EPO’s focus on aligning procedural innovations with industrial user needs. The rising SME participation in the Unitary Patent system suggests fee reductions are influencing filing behavior. For practitioners, the emphasis on human-centric AI signals that automation will augment rather than replace examiner judgment, preserving current quality expectations while enhancing efficiency.

items.title: Presidium announces establishment of a second panel at Düsseldorf Local Division
items.title_zh: UPC主席团宣布杜塞尔多夫地方法院设立第二合议庭
items.title_en: UPC Presidium Establishes Second Panel at Düsseldorf Local Division Effective March 2026
items.summary: The Presidium of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has decided the establishment of a second panel at Düsseldorf Local Division (DE) as of 1 March 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）主席团于2026年1月20日作出决定，自2026年3月1日起在杜塞尔多夫地方法院设立第二合议庭。贝雷尼斯·托姆法官被任命为第二合议庭审判长，英戈·林肯法官加入该庭。第一合议庭由罗尼·托马斯法官任审判长，朱勒·舒马赫法官加入。该决定依据UPCA及程序规则作出，旨在优化案件审理能力。
items.ai_summary_en: The Presidium of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has decided to establish a second panel at the Düsseldorf Local Division, effective 1 March 2026. This decision, adopted on 20 January 2026 under Article 8 of the UPCA and Rule 341.4 of the Rules of Procedure, aims to enhance judicial capacity. Ms Bérénice Thom is appointed as Presiding Judge of Panel 2, joined by legally qualified judge Mr Ingo Rinken. Panel 1 remains presided over by Mr Ronny Thomas, with Ms Jule Schumacher serving as the second legally qualified judge. The expansion reflects ongoing adjustments to manage caseloads in one of the court’s busiest divisions.
items.ai_insight_en: This structural expansion addresses capacity needs in Düsseldorf, a key venue for European patent litigation. Rights holders should note that case allocation mechanisms may evolve to distribute work between the two panels. Monitoring the composition of these panels is essential for understanding potential judicial approaches in upcoming proceedings.

items.title: UPC monthly statistics publication
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院恢复发布月度统计数据
items.title_en: Unified Patent Court Resumes Monthly Statistics with Updated Format and New Data Sets
items.summary: The Unified Patent Court resumes the publication of its monthly statistics
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院宣布恢复发布月度统计数据，并推出更新格式以提供更清晰洞察。截至2026年2月28日，一审法院累计受理1130起案件。2026年前两月新立案72起，其中侵权诉讼30起，撤销反诉39起。杜塞尔多夫、慕尼黑和海牙分院案件量居前。英语为最主要诉讼语言，占比62.5%。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court has resumed publishing monthly statistics, introducing an updated format for clearer insights and incorporating new data sets. As of 28 February 2026, the Court of First Instance recorded 1,130 total cases since June 2023. In 2026, 72 new cases were filed, comprising 30 infringement actions and 39 revocation counterclaims. The data highlights continued activity across key divisions, with Düsseldorf, Munich, and The Hague showing significant caseloads. This relaunch aims to provide a more comprehensive overview of the Court’s operational trends and procedural distributions for stakeholders monitoring European patent litigation developments.
items.ai_insight_en: The resumption of regular statistical reporting enhances transparency for rights holders and counsel tracking UPC litigation trends. The data confirms the continued centrality of specific divisions, particularly in Germany and the Netherlands, for patent disputes. Practitioners should note the high ratio of revocation counterclaims relative to standalone actions, reflecting common defensive strategies. Monitoring these updated metrics helps inform forum selection and resource allocation for ongoing or prospective European patent enforcement and defense matters.

items.title: President’s mission to Hungary
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局局长访问匈牙利
items.title_en: EPO President Visits Hungary to Discuss European Patent Network Modernization
items.summary: President Campinos attended the 130th anniversary of the Hungarian Intellectual Property Office (HIPO) and met with HIPO President Szabolcs Farkas to discuss the future of the European Patent Network.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局局长坎皮诺斯出席匈牙利知识产权局成立130周年庆典，并与该局主席法卡斯会晤。双方讨论了国家和欧洲层面的知识产权优先事项，重点涉及加强欧洲专利网络、通过统一专利体系深化整合、协调实践及共享数字工具，以提升欧洲技术市场的连贯性与韧性。
items.ai_summary_en: EPO President António Campinos attended the 130th anniversary of the Hungarian Intellectual Property Office (HIPO) and met with HIPO President Szabolcs Farkas. Discussions focused on national and European IP priorities, emphasizing the need for high quality and legal certainty amid technological change. The visit supported the EPO’s ongoing consultations for its new Scenarios for the Future study. Key topics included strengthening the European Patent Network through deeper integration via the Unitary Patent system, harmonized practices, and shared digital tools. Both leaders highlighted the importance of modernizing cooperation to enhance capacity-building and joint support for startups and SMEs, aiming to reinforce Europe as a coherent and resilient technology market.
items.ai_insight_en: This engagement signals the EPO’s continued push for deeper integration within the European Patent Network, particularly leveraging the Unitary Patent system. For rights holders, the emphasis on harmonized practices and shared digital tools suggests potential improvements in procedural consistency and efficiency across member states. Monitoring the outcomes of the Scenarios for the Future study will be essential for understanding long-term strategic shifts in the European innovation ecosystem.

items.title: EPO meets with Novartis
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局与诺华举行双边会议
items.title_en: EPO and Novartis Discuss Examination Timeliness and Centralised SPC System
items.summary: On 4 February, the European Patent Office (EPO) held an online meeting with the Swiss pharmaceutical and biotechnology company Novartis.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年2月4日，欧洲专利局与诺华举行在线会议，这是双方深化用户对话倡议以来的第110次双边会晤。诺华强调专利审查时效对投资决策至关重要，并对统一专利持谨慎积极态度，强烈支持由统一专利法院处理无效请求和上诉的集中式补充保护证书制度。双方还讨论了抗体相关专利申请审查指南的最新修订及公众咨询安排。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office held its fourth bilateral meeting of 2026 with Novartis, focusing on structured user dialogue. Novartis emphasized that timely patent examination is critical for investment decisions regarding new formulations and further medical uses. The company expressed cautious support for the Unitary Patent and strong backing for a centralised supplementary protection certificate system, preferably with Unified Patent Court jurisdiction for invalidity and appeals. Discussions also covered recent revisions to examination guidelines for antibody-related applications. The EPO highlighted the annual public consultation process for guideline updates, inviting stakeholder feedback before the April entry into force.
items.ai_insight_en: This dialogue underscores the industry's demand for procedural efficiency and legal certainty in Europe. Support for a centralised SPC system highlights ongoing efforts to streamline post-grant protections. Applicants should leverage public consultations to influence examination practices, particularly in complex biotechnology fields like antibodies, ensuring guidelines reflect practical prosecution realities.

items.title_en: UPC Administrative Committee Adopts PMAC Procedural Rules for Mediation and Arbitration
items.summary: The procedural rules of the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) were formally approved and adopted by the Administrative Committee on the 24 April 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月24日，统一专利法院（UPC）行政委员会正式批准专利调解与仲裁中心（PMAC）的程序规则。PMAC将提供调解、仲裁及专家裁定等争议解决服务，涵盖欧洲专利、单一专利及相关跨境纠纷，包括SEP/FRAND案件。预计5月启动调解服务，年内陆续开放其他服务。此举标志着UPC体系下替代性争议解决机制的正式确立，为权利人提供了诉讼之外的多元化纠纷处理路径。
items.ai_summary_en: On 24 April 2026, the Administrative Committee of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) formally approved the procedural rules for the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC). This adoption enables PMAC to provide dispute resolution services including mediation, arbitration, expert determination, and hybrid processes. The Centre will handle disputes concerning European and Unitary Patents, as well as related cross-border matters and SEP/FRAND cases. Mediation services are scheduled to commence in May 2026, with arbitration and expert determination services to follow later in the year. Full texts of the rules and fee schedules are available on the PMAC and UPC websites.
items.ai_insight_en: The establishment of PMAC's operational framework provides rights holders with structured non-litigation pathways within the UPC ecosystem. For portfolio managers, this introduces viable options for resolving SEP/FRAND negotiations and cross-border conflicts outside traditional court proceedings. Monitoring the rollout of these services is essential for understanding how alternative dispute resolution may complement or alter current enforcement strategies in Europe.

items.title: PMAC's Case Management System now live
items.title_zh: PMAC案件管理系统正式上线
items.title_en: UPC's PMAC Launches Case Management System for Mediation and Arbitration
items.summary: As of today, 12 May 2026, the Case Management System of the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) is live
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月12日，统一专利法院（UPC）宣布其专利调解与仲裁中心（PMAC）的案件管理系统（CCMS）正式启用。该系统支持用户在线提交替代性争议解决（ADR）信息及调解程序请求。PMAC网站已设立专门页面提供系统使用指南。此举标志着UPC在优化专利纠纷非诉讼解决机制方面迈出重要一步，为当事人提供了更便捷的调解与仲裁服务渠道，有助于提高争议解决效率并降低诉讼成本。
items.ai_summary_en: As of 12 May 2026, the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) has officially launched its Case Management System (CCMS). This digital platform enables parties to submit requests for ADR information sessions and mediation proceedings directly through the Centre. The PMAC website now features a dedicated section providing practical guidance on navigating the new system. This development marks the operational availability of structured alternative dispute resolution services under the Unified Patent Court framework, offering a streamlined digital interface for initiating non-litigious patent dispute resolutions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇专利纠纷时，应关注并利用PMAC的调解与仲裁机制。相比UPC诉讼，ADR程序通常更具成本效益且保密性强。建议企业熟悉CCMS操作流程，在争议初期评估通过调解快速和解的可能性，以规避高昂的跨国诉讼风险并维护商业关系。
items.ai_insight_en: The launch of the PMAC Case Management System provides rights holders with a formalized digital channel for accessing alternative dispute resolution mechanisms within the UPC ecosystem. For in-house counsel and IP teams managing European portfolios, this infrastructure supports efficient initiation of mediation or arbitration without immediate recourse to full litigation. Monitoring the uptake and procedural rules of this system will help stakeholders evaluate its utility for resolving cross-border patent disputes efficiently.

items.title: Upcoming changes to judicial compositions of the Court of Appeal and the Court of First Instance, Central Division Paris
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉院及巴黎分院法官组成变更
items.title_en: UPC Announces Judicial Composition Changes for Court of Appeal and Paris Central Division
items.summary: The Court informs on changes to the judicial compositions of the Court of Appeal and Court of First Instance, Central Division Paris, effective as of 1 April 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院宣布，自2026年4月1日起调整上诉法院及第一审法院巴黎中央分院的法官组成。因Emanuela Germano法官退休，Paolo Catallozzi法官调任上诉法院，Vincenzo Carnì法官接任巴黎中央分院。该决定由行政委员会于2025年7月作出。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court has announced changes to the judicial compositions of the Court of Appeal and the Court of First Instance, Central Division Paris, effective 1 April 2026. These adjustments follow the retirement of legally qualified judge Ms Emanuela Germano from the Court of Appeal at the end of March 2026. The Administrative Committee, via decisions dated 2 July 2025, appointed Mr Paolo Catallozzi, previously a judge at the Central Division Paris, to the Court of Appeal. To fill the resulting vacancy, Mr Vincenzo Carnì has been appointed as a legally qualified judge at the Court of First Instance, Central Division Paris. The Court presidents acknowledged Ms Germano’s contributions and welcomed the new appointees.
items.ai_insight_en: This administrative update confirms the continuity of judicial operations within key UPC divisions. For practitioners, noting the transition of Judge Catallozzi to the appellate level and the introduction of Judge Carnì in Paris is essential for tracking panel compositions in pending or future cases. While specific jurisprudential trends cannot be inferred from appointment notices alone, rights holders should update internal records to reflect these changes in judicial rosters for strategic case assessment.

items.title: CoA, May 15, 2026, Order On Request For Use Of English At Oral Hearing, UPC-CoA-50/2026
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭准许口头听证使用英语的裁定
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Permits English Oral Pleadings Despite French Language of Proceedings
items.summary: The appellants’ representative understood French but lacked sufficient oral proficiency for optimal representation. The Court permitted both parties to plead in English at the hearing of 26 May 2026, while French remained the language of proceedings.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在Robert Bosch诉Valeo案中作出裁定，尽管法语为案件程序语言，但鉴于上诉人代表法语口语能力不足且被上诉人同意，法院基于程序效率与公平原则，准许双方在2026年5月26日的口头听证中使用英语进行辩论。法院引用先例及程序规则前言作为法律依据，强调灵活性与成本效益，未正式变更案件语言。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal authorized oral pleadings in English for a hearing where French remained the formal language of proceedings. The decision addressed an appellant’s representative who understood French but lacked sufficient oral proficiency for effective advocacy. The court prioritized procedural efficiency and fairness, noting that direct English advocacy was superior to using an interpreter. Crucially, the respondent consented to the request, which strengthened the application. The court relied on the Rules of Procedure Preamble’s principles of flexibility and cost-effectiveness, citing prior procedural orders involving the same parties as persuasive precedent for maintaining consistency in case management.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁定确立了UPC上诉庭在特定条件下允许庭审语言与程序语言分离的实务操作。当事人可通过证明语言障碍、获得对方同意并援引效率原则，申请使用更熟练的语言进行口头辩论。这为跨国诉讼中的语言策略提供了灵活性，同时强调了双方合意对程序决定的关键影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This order highlights the UPC’s willingness to decouple the language of oral hearings from the formal language of proceedings to ensure effective representation. It demonstrates that party consensus on procedural logistics can drive judicial discretion, particularly when supported by principles of efficiency. Practitioners should note that prior procedural history between identical parties may influence subsequent case management decisions, offering a pathway for predictable handling of language barriers in multinational litigation.

items.title: Preliminary Objections Before The UPC: A Clear Boundary Between Jurisdiction And Substance
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院初步异议：管辖权与实体问题的明确界限
items.title_en: UPC Preliminary Objections: Strict Boundary Between Jurisdiction and Substance
items.summary: What do the Rules of Procedure of the Unified Patent Court stipulate about raising a preliminary objection and providing a response to such objections? Sofiane Belhadj-Kacem provides the answer in...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章分析统一专利法院在REEL International诉Fives ECL案中的裁定。法院依据《程序规则》第19.1条，驳回被告基于诉讼利益和德国判决既判力提出的初步异议。法院明确初步异议仅限管辖权、分庭管辖权及语言三类情形，其他实体争议应合并审理以符合程序经济原则。
items.ai_summary_en: In a dispute between REEL International and Fives ECL, the Unified Patent Court rejected a defendant’s preliminary objection based on lack of interest to sue and res judicata from German proceedings. Relying on Rule 19.1 of the Rules of Procedure, the Court clarified that preliminary objections are strictly limited to challenges regarding court jurisdiction, division competence, or language of proceedings. The Court held that issues such as standing and res judicata require substantive examination of facts and evidence, rather than serving as procedural filters. This decision reinforces a clear structural distinction between early jurisdictional checks and full merit-based adjudication within the UPC framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: UPC通过严格界定初步异议范围，防止程序碎片化，强调诉讼经济与快速裁决。当事人需注意管辖权异议与实体抗辩的程序分流，避免试图通过初步异议拖延实体审理。此裁判思路有助于确立UPC高效审理的司法惯例。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling confirms the UPC’s commitment to procedural efficiency by preventing the fragmentation of cases through broad preliminary objections. Practitioners should note that defenses like res judicata will be addressed during merits proceedings, not as threshold jurisdictional bars. This approach reduces delays but requires parties to prepare comprehensive factual arguments early in the litigation process.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 19.1 limits preliminary objections exclusively to jurisdiction, division competence, and language issues.", "Claims regarding interest to sue and res judicata fall outside the scope of preliminary objections.", "Substantive defenses require full factual examination and cannot be resolved via procedural dismissal."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确初步异议的封闭列表性质，排除诉讼利益等实体前置抗辩", "确立合并审理原则，拒绝将既判力等问题从实体审理中剥离", "观察UPC后续案件是否持续贯彻这一效率优先的程序管理逻辑"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Expect faster initial jurisdictional rulings, but anticipate that substantive defenses like res judicata will proceed to full trial rather than causing early dis…", "Practice note: Do not rely on Rule 19.1 for non-jurisdictional defenses; prepare detailed factual evidence for merits hearings instead of seeking procedural shortcuts.", "What to monitor: Future UPC case law to confirm if this strict interpretation of Rule 19.1 remains consistent across different divisions and fact patterns."]

Claims regarding interest to sue and res judicata fall outside the scope of preliminary objections.
Substantive defenses require full factual examination and cannot be resolved via procedural dismissal.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Expect faster initial jurisdictional rulings, but anticipate that substantive defenses like res judicata will proceed to full trial rather than causing early dis…
Practice note: Do not rely on Rule 19.1 for non-jurisdictional defenses; prepare detailed factual evidence for merits hearings instead of seeking procedural shortcuts.
What to monitor: Future UPC case law to confirm if this strict interpretation of Rule 19.1 remains consistent across different divisions and fact patterns.

items.title: LD Brussels, May 7, 2026, Cost Decision, UPC_CFI_2265/2025
items.title_zh: UPC布鲁塞尔地院：权利放弃需明示及费用裁定要点
items.title_en: UPC Brussels: Waiver Requires Explicit Statement and Cost Calculation Rules
items.summary: Waiver of rights requires an explicit statement – prior admissions in first instance proceedings do not constitute a waiver (R. 150 RoP)
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC布鲁塞尔地方法院在Yealink案费用裁定中明确：权利放弃需 explicit 声明，一审承认对方胜诉不构成放弃索赔权；部分胜诉与费用抵销不可双重适用以降低上限；当事人自行安排的翻译费不可回收；临时禁令后提起实体诉讼时，费用申请期限自实体判决送达起算。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of First Instance in Brussels issued a cost decision clarifying that waiving rights requires an explicit statement. Prior admissions regarding the opposing party’s success in first instance proceedings do not constitute a waiver of the right to claim compensation as a partially successful party under Rule 150 RoP. The Court ruled that partial success reductions and cost set-offs cannot be applied simultaneously to reduce the applicable ceiling, preventing a double penalty. Additionally, interpreter costs arranged independently by a party are excluded from recoverable costs, which are limited to those arranged by the Court. The one-month deadline for cost applications runs from the merits decision when substantive proceedings follow provisional measures.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁定细化了UPC费用分摊规则，强调权利放弃的明示要求及翻译费回收限制。实务中需注意费用申请起算点随实体诉讼启动而延后，且应避免对同一费用项主张双重扣减，以符合比例原则。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision provides essential guidance on UPC cost allocation, emphasizing strict formal requirements for waiving rights and prohibiting double reductions in cost ceilings. It clarifies recoverability limits for interpretation services and confirms that cost application deadlines align with final merits decisions in multi-stage litigation, aiding predictable budgeting and procedural planning for rights holders operating across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["权利放弃需明确声明，一审中承认对方为胜诉方不构成后续费用程序中放弃部分胜诉索赔权", "部分胜诉降低费用上限与具体费用抵销不可同时适用，法院维持全额上限并直接扣除合理费用", "当事人依规则自行安排的同声传译费用不属于可回收诉讼费用，仅法院安排的翻译费可回收"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Waiver of rights requires clear, explicit declaration; prior procedural admissions do not suffice.", "Partial success adjustments and cost set-offs cannot both reduce the applicable cost ceiling.", "Independently arranged interpreter costs are non-recoverable; only Court-arranged interpretation is compensable."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确权利放弃必须基于清晰、明确的声明，默示行为或程序性承认不产生放弃效力", "自行聘请翻译产生的费用无法转嫁给对方，需在预算阶段予以内部消化", "关注临时禁令与实体诉讼并行时，费用申请期限从实体判决送达日起算的程序细节"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Parties must explicitly declare any waiver of rights to avoid unintended loss of cost recovery opportunities in subsequent proceedings.", "Practice note: Do not assume prior acknowledgments of success waive cost claims; ensure interpreter services are Court-arranged if cost recovery is anticipated.", "What to monitor: Track how UPC courts apply the prohibition on double-penalty cost reductions and interpret proportionality in provisional measure appeals."]
core_points_en: Waiver of rights requires clear, explicit declaration; prior procedural admissions do not suffice.
Partial success adjustments and cost set-offs cannot both reduce the applicable cost ceiling.
Independently arranged interpreter costs are non-recoverable; only Court-arranged interpretation is compensable.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Parties must explicitly declare any waiver of rights to avoid unintended loss of cost recovery opportunities in subsequent proceedings.
Practice note: Do not assume prior acknowledgments of success waive cost claims; ensure interpreter services are Court-arranged if cost recovery is anticipated.
What to monitor: Track how UPC courts apply the prohibition on double-penalty cost reductions and interpret proportionality in provisional measure appeals.

items.title: LD Milan, May 5, 2026, Decision, UPC_CFI_727/2024
items.title_zh: 米兰地院/UPC判决：审查档案指导权利要求解释
items.title_en: UPC Milan: Prosecution History Guides Claim Interpretation and Formstein Defence Applies
items.summary: Prosecution file statements before the EPO, while not binding, may guide claim interpretation as reflecting the skilled person’s view (Art. 69 EPC, Art. 24(1)(c) UPCA)
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC第一审法院在米兰作出的判决指出，EPO审查档案中的陈述虽无约束力，但可反映本领域技术人员观点以指导权利要求解释。法院据此对“抵接”作狭义解释，既确认新颖性破坏又判定不侵权。此外，迟交实物证据因违反前置程序原则被拒，且被告因产品对应现有技术而免除侵权责任。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of First Instance in Milan ruled that statements made during EPO prosecution, while not binding, guide claim interpretation under Article 69 EPC. The court adopted a narrow construction of abutment based on the patentee’s prior assertions, resulting in findings of both novelty-destroying prior art and non-infringement. Late-submitted physical evidence was rejected as inadmissible due to violations of front-loaded procedural rules and lack of provenance. Furthermore, the court applied the Formstein defence, excluding infringement where the accused product mirrored prior art embodiments, despite the patent’s validity in amended form.

items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces the strategic importance of consistency between prosecution positions and litigation arguments. It highlights the UPC’s strict adherence to front-loaded evidence rules and the operational significance of the Formstein defence in separating validity from infringement outcomes.

items.title_zh: UPC赫尔辛基分院：专利侵权与有效性裁决及等同原则适用
items.title_en: UPC Helsinki Applies Hague Equivalence Approach and Limits Revocation Scope in EP 3 295 663 Decision
items.summary: The Helsinki Local Division of the Unified Patent Court examined infringement proceedings involving EP 3 295 663, with AIM Sport Development AG asserting patent rights against multiple TGI Sport entities across European jurisdictions. The case included a counterclaim for revocation, requiring the court to analyze validity under Articles 54, 56, and 123(2) EPC alongside infringement claims under Article 69 EPC.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）赫尔辛基地方分院就EP 3 295 663号专利作出侵权与有效性判决。在缺乏上诉法院先例时，法院采纳海牙分院的等同侵权判定路径，强调等同标准需累积满足，任一不成立即无侵权。针对撤销反诉，法院指出若当事人未充分论证“新增事项”，仅需回应已 substantiated 的部分。该案明确了UPC内部司法一致性的重要性及举证责任边界。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court's Helsinki Local Division issued a decision on infringement and validity concerning European patent EP 3 295 663, held by AIM Sport Development AG against multiple TGI Sport entities. In the absence of Court of Appeal precedent on equivalence, the Division adopted the approach established by The Hague Local Division, emphasizing that equivalence criteria are cumulative; failure to meet any single criterion precludes a finding of infringement. Regarding the counterclaim for revocation based on added matter under Article 123(2) EPC, the Court ruled it would only address arguments substantiated by the defendant, noting that TGI provided minimal detail and did not further develop this allegation during oral proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需密切关注各地方分院对等同原则的司法倾向，尤其是海牙、赫尔辛基等关键分院。在提出无效抗辩时，必须提供详尽的技术比对与法律论证，避免仅凭形式主张导致被驳回。建议建立跨法域的专利风险预警机制，统一应诉策略以应对UPC日益增强的判例约束力。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces the importance of judicial consistency across UPC Local Divisions, particularly regarding the doctrine of equivalents. It signals that applicants and defendants must provide robust, substantiated technical and legal arguments for invalidity defenses, as courts will not expand the scope of review beyond what is rigorously pleaded. This highlights the need for precise drafting of counterclaims and careful selection of equivalence frameworks in litigation strategy.
